summary: The session/event OTL request family is now tied to two runtime global roots: 0x140C4C948 is a broader launcher/client configuration root initialized through a runescape bootstrap path, and 0x140C4C9C8 is a login/TOTP lifecycle root that is passed directly into session/open and event request builders.
confidence: medium-high for global read/write sites, producer/clearer functions, direct OTL consumers, and runtime-zero data layout; medium for final high-level class names because no RTTI/class metadata has been assigned yet.

primary_globals.role: Runtime-initialized launcher/client configuration or service root used by the OTL session helpers and by wider bootstrap/client paths.

evidence: Checks the global for null at 0x140020289, allocates through 0x140793B40, stores the new pointer at 0x1400202C0, and cleans the old pointer through 0x14002A6D0 plus 0x1407B8478.
Initializes fields on the allocated object and assigns the literal runescape through 0x140B5E030.
The same function also references the format literal %s:%u, consistent with bootstrap or endpoint/client identity formatting.
Loads the previous global at 0x14002570E, destroys/frees it, stores R15 into 0x140C4C948 at 0x140025733, and calls 0x1407B5260 afterward.

otl_consumer_sites.evidence: Loads 0x140C4C948 at 0x1406294AB and calls 0x140277AA0 with RCX based on owner +0xB0 and RDX loaded from +0xB8, linking the global to the session owner update/copy path.
Session sync/open coordinator reads this global at 0x1406295AB and 0x140629955 while composing the lastFullSync/lastCardUpdate query and dispatching session/open.
Additional nearby OTL/session helper functions read the same global, making it a shared root for the local session helper family rather than a single request field.

classification_notes: This global should not be named as an OTL request object. It is a longer-lived root object consumed by OTL helpers.
Because it is also read by non-OTL bootstrap/client code, keep the name broad until the allocation layout is fully typed.

primary_globals.role: Runtime-initialized login/session service root that gates the OTL session/event path and is produced by the login/TOTP setup lifecycle.

evidence: References login-related literals totp, login, and atotp.
Compares the current global at 0x140152CE7, loads the previous pointer at 0x140152D19, stores the replacement pointer at 0x140152D20, then calls lifecycle helpers around the replacement.
Loads the current global at 0x140152DD6, passes it through the same lifecycle helper family, and clears 0x140C4C9C8 to zero at 0x140152DE5.

otl_consumer_sites.evidence: The session sync/open coordinator loads RCX from 0x140C4C9C8 at 0x140629939 immediately before calling the session/open request builder 0x14062A760. RDX points at the stack-composed endpoint/query string and R8 carries caller-provided session/user material.
The event request builder loads 0x140C4C9C8 at 0x14062B21A while composing the event endpoint %s/%s/%s/%s?userHash=%s and then builds the shared 0x268-byte request object.
A nearby OTL/session helper also reads 0x140C4C9C8 at 0x140629CFA, keeping this global inside the same request/session neighborhood.

classification_notes: This root is closer to auth/login lifecycle than telemetry-only state. Session open/close/event traffic appears to depend on login/TOTP setup being active.
OpenNXT compatibility work should expect session/event OTL traffic to share lifecycle ownership with login setup rather than being a detached telemetry singleton.

adjacent_globals.assessment: Broad runtime pointer with many unrelated consumers. It sits near the session roots but should not be named OTL-specific yet.
Adjacent runtime pointer with mixed subsystem consumers. It is not currently proven to be part of the OTL request/session family.

data_layout_observations: The qword window 0x140C4C930..0x140C4C9D8 is file-zero for the key pointer slots, confirming these roots are populated at runtime.

The same data neighborhood contains static float-like defaults including 0x3FF0000000000000 and several 0x3F800000 values, so adjacency alone is not enough to classify nearby globals as session state.

Direct branch-target scanning found no decoded branch instructions targeting the session/open, session/close, or event builder entry addresses 0x14062A760, 0x14062AC81, and 0x14062B170; their currently mapped use is through direct call edges rather than tail-branch entry aliases.
opennxt_947_server_implications: Session/open and event request construction is downstream of the login/TOTP lifecycle root 0x140C4C9C8, so server-side compatibility traces should correlate launcher login state with OTL session traffic.

The client has a broader runescape bootstrap/config root at 0x140C4C948 that session helpers consult alongside the login/session root. Server and tooling notes should avoid treating session telemetry as a standalone module.

The previous request-object mapping remains intact: these roots feed the already-mapped 0x268-byte request object family, constructor 0x14062B7B0, cleanup 0x14062B8C0, and submitters 0x14062BCE0/0x14062BC20.
reverser_tool_usage: pe-address-refs isolated runtime global readers and writers for 0x140C4C948, 0x140C4C9C8, 0x140C4C9C0, and 0x140C4C9D0.
pe-read-qwords confirmed file-image zero values and nearby static defaults for the .data window.
pe-function-calls, pe-callsite-registers, pe-function-literals, and pe-instructions provided the call-chain, argument, literal, and instruction evidence used here.

No tool code change was made in this milestone because the existing Reverser commands already captured the reusable workflow cleanly. The next tool improvement candidate is a small report/export command that condenses address-ref, literal, and callsite-register artifacts into this style of global-root summary.
next_targets: Type the allocation layout for the object stored in 0x140C4C948, especially fields initialized by 0x14002026E and replacement path 0x1400254D0.
Map login/TOTP lifecycle helper pair 0x14016A920 and 0x14016A7C0 to explain object ownership around 0x140C4C9C8 replacement and clearing.
Follow additional 0x140C4C9C8 readers in 0x14015407E, 0x140156450, 0x140159350, and 0x1401F65F0 to connect login UI/auth state transitions with OTL session availability.
Add a Reverser report exporter that merges global address refs, function literals, callsite registers, and qword data into a compact global-root summary scaffold.
